title: Agentic VOC Insight
summary: Agentic VOC Insight is an automated intelligence pipeline that leverages multi-agent AI systems to transform raw Voice of Customer (VOC) data into structured, actionable insights. By orchestrating specialized agents for sentiment analysis, trend identification, and thematic categorization, the project provides a scalable solution for interpreting large volumes of unstructured feedback.

title: Agentic Scorecard
summary: Agentic Scorecard is an automated framework designed to modernize credit risk modeling by integrating Large Language Models (LLMs) with human in the loop. The repository features an end-to-end pipeline that utilizes agentic workflows to automate scorecard development, putting human as supervisor (approver), and produce high-accuracy scorecards that meet strict regulatory standards for interpretability. By utilizing AI as a scorecard designer and human supervisor, this project provides a transparent and scalable solution for developing production-grade credit scoring systems.

title: RAG Quran Kemenag
summary: A Python-based Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) project that turns Indonesian Quran and tafseer data into a searchable, AI-powered chatbot workflow: it cleans and translates source content, builds structured vector datasets (translation, tafseer, and surah summaries), and indexes them into ChromaDB for semantic retrieval, then uses Gemini + LangChain to answer user questions in a terminal chat interface.

title: Telegram Trading Shaman
summary: A Python-based Telegram bot project designed to monitor trading signals and automatically notify group members when actionable market conditions are detected, helping traders react faster without constant manual chart watching. The repository packages a lightweight alerting workflow with dependency management and Docker support for easier deployment.

title: Alpha Vantage Data Logger in GCP
summary: a small Python data pipeline that pulls cryptocurrency market data from the Alpha Vantage API and lands it in Google Cloud Storage, organized by ticker (with a configurable symbol list). The project is meant to run on Google Cloud as a containerized workload: you build a Docker image, deploy it as a Cloud Run Job, and trigger it on a schedule with Cloud Scheduler (using authenticated HTTP calls), so price snapshots are collected reliably without manual runs. It is a practical example of cloud-native ingestion—secrets via environment variables, IAM for job invocation, and repeatable GCP setup steps for a personal analytics or trading-data stack.

title: Credit Risk App in Streamlit
summary: a Streamlit-based Python application for building classic credit-risk scorecards with logistic regression: you upload tabular data, choose features, split into train and test sets, and explore both automated and manual Weight-of-Evidence (WoE) binning. It bundles practical model-development utilities—information value and correlation-driven feature screening, L2-regularized hyperparameter tuning, and tooling to translate the fitted model into an interpretable scorecard plus SQL-friendly artifacts—so analysts can go from raw borrower attributes to a deployable scoring view in one interactive workflow.
